calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
David Cook, Esq. (415-989-4730; Cook@SqueezeBloodFromTurnip.com) of Cook Collection Attorneys for Plaintiff.
Haroon Manjlai, Esq. (951-268-4384; hm@manjlailaw.com) of Manjlai Law for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ABC LANDSCAPING & EXCAVATION, INC.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND:

Defendant ABC Landscape & Excavation Inc.'s motion to vacate default and default judgment is denied.

First, the motion is untimely. Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) requires that defendant bring this motion "within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months" from entry of the default. Here, the default was entered on October 1, 2020, and defendant did not file this motion until June 9, 2021. The record demonstrates that defendant had sufficient notice to defend this action but did not act with diligence. Defendant enlisted the aid of out-of-state counsel and plaintiff warned him on September 23, 2020 that a default would be entered. (Cook Decl., Exs. R, S.) Defendant also retained California counsel who was aware of the action by January 29, 2021. (Cook Decl., Ex. A.) Moreover, defendant was aware of the December 7, 2020 judgment within two days after it was entered. (Id., Ex. T.) There is no adequate explanation for defendant's lengthy delay in seeking relief. (See Carter Decl.) A three-month delay without an adequate explanation is unreasonable as a matter of law. (See Minick v. City of Petaluma (2016) 3 Cal.App.5th 15, 34.)

Second, the cursory Carter declaration does not establish a satisfactory excuse for failing to timely respond to the complaint.

Third, defendant is a suspended corporation that cannot defend this action. (See Friends of Shingle Springs Interchange, Inc. v. County of El Dorado (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1470, 1486; Tabarrejo v. Superior Court (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 849, 861-862; Cook Decl., Exs. W and X.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964. =(302/EPS)
